penalties.description: Task was not completed within the 300 second time limit or correct task setup was not identified.

consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement between models on task identification. Claude identified this as wrong task setup (peg transfer instead of precision cutting), while GPT-4o scored it as completed precision cutting. Given the fundamental disagreement on task type, the conservative approach is to follow Claude's assessment of incorrect task setup, resulting in zero score for incomplete task.

disagreements.rationale: Claude's identification of wrong task setup takes precedence; if this is not the correct task, completion time is irrelevant and defaults to timeout.

claude_value.description: Task was not completed within the 300 second time limit. The marked circle was not fully cut out.

gpt_value.description: The cut deviates slightly inside the marked circle.

disagreements.rationale: Fundamental task identification disagreement requires conservative approach. If wrong task setup, then incomplete_circle penalty is appropriate.
If task was not completed or wrong setup identified, circle_release phase would not be achieved.
Conservative approach given fundamental disagreement on task completion and setup identification.
